description: Implement stdout output plugin — simplest output, validates Output interface with real pipeline
steps: Create src/plugins/outputs/stdout.ts
Implement Zod config schema (format: json | line_protocol)
Implement connect() as no-op
Implement write(batch): format each metric and console.log()
Implement JSON format: name, tags, fields, timestamp as JSON object
Implement line_protocol format: Telegraf-compatible line protocol string
Implement close() as no-op
Write unit tests
Register plugin in plugin registry
verification: Stdout output prints metrics in correct format. All tests pass.
tests: write() outputs JSON representation of each metric
write() with line_protocol format outputs Telegraf-compatible strings
connect() and close() don't throw
Batch of 10 metrics → 10 output lines
Metric with tags and multiple fields serialised correctly in JSON
Metric with special characters in names/tags handled correctly

description: Implement file output plugin — JSON-lines and CSV formats, append mode
steps: Create src/plugins/outputs/file.ts
Implement Zod config schema (path, format: json | csv)
Implement connect(): open/create file in append mode
Implement write(batch): serialise and append metrics
Implement JSON-lines format: one JSON object per line
Implement CSV format: header on first write, data rows after
Implement close(): flush and close file handle
Handle write errors (disk full, permissions) gracefully
Write unit tests
verification: File output writes correct JSON-lines and CSV. Append mode works. All tests pass.
tests: JSON format: write batch → file contains valid JSON-lines (one per metric)
CSV format: write batch → file has header row + data rows
Append mode: two write() calls → all metrics in file (not overwritten)
connect() creates file if it doesn't exist
connect() appends to existing file (doesn't truncate)
close() flushes buffered data
Write error (invalid path) → error propagated, not swallowed
Empty batch → no error, nothing written
Metric fields of all types (number, bigint, string, boolean) serialised correctly

description: Integration test: file output in pipeline — metrics flow from input through pipeline to file
steps: Create test/integration/file-pipeline.test.ts
Configure pipeline with mock input + file output (JSON-lines)
Verify metrics written to file match what input produced
Verify global tags present in file output
Test with processor in pipeline — transformed metrics in file
verification: Integration test passes. Pipeline metrics arrive in file correctly.
tests: Mock input → pipeline → file output: JSON-lines file contains correct metrics
Global tags present in file output metrics
Processor transforms reflected in file output

description: Implement local data store output — SQLite with daily rotation, retention, batch writes, MessagePack encoding
steps: Create src/plugins/outputs/local-store.ts
Implement Zod config schema matching PRD §11
Implement connect(): create data directory, open/create today's daily DB, create tables, set PRAGMAs (WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=5000)
Implement daily file routing: getDailyDatabase() returns correct DB for current date
Implement SQLite schema: metrics table + tag_index table per PRD §11
Implement write(batch): BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction, batch INSERT with MessagePack-encoded fields, update tag_index with upsert
Implement MessagePack encoding/decoding of metric fields via msgpackr
Implement tags_hash using metric's hashId() (FNV-64a)
Implement quality mapping: good=0, uncertain=1, bad=2
Implement retention: time-based (delete old daily files), size-based (evict oldest when exceeding limit)
Implement downsampling: aggregate old data to min/max/mean/count per interval
Implement CSV export: query time range across daily files → CSV output
Implement startup recovery: WAL checkpoint + integrity check on open
Implement close(): WAL checkpoint, close all open DB handles
Implement SQLITE_BUSY retry (one retry)
Write comprehensive tests
verification: Local store persists and retrieves metrics correctly. Retention works. All tests pass.
tests: write() inserts metrics into daily SQLite file with correct schema
Metrics retrievable: query by time range returns correct data
MessagePack round-trip: all FieldValue types encode and decode correctly
Daily rotation: metrics on different UTC days go to different files
Tag index populated: name + tags_hash entries created on write
Tag index upsert: last_seen updated on subsequent writes for same series
Retention time-based: daily files older than retention_days are deleted
Retention size-based: oldest daily file deleted when total exceeds retention_max_gb
WAL mode enabled on new databases (PRAGMA journal_mode check)
Startup recovery: WAL checkpoint runs on connect()
Batch write atomicity: all-or-nothing transaction
SQLITE_BUSY: handled gracefully with retry
CSV export: correct headers and data for time range
Empty write (no metrics) → no error, no empty transaction
connect() creates data directory if missing
close() checkpoints WAL and closes handles
Midnight boundary: metric at 23:59:59 UTC → today's file, 00:00:00 → tomorrow's file
Downsampling: 60 one-second points → 1 summary with correct min/max/mean/count

description: Integration test: local store in pipeline — full data path from input to SQLite
steps: Create test/integration/local-store-pipeline.test.ts
Configure pipeline with mock input + local store output
Verify metrics persisted to SQLite with correct structure
Verify MessagePack fields decode correctly from stored data
Verify tag_index populated
Verify global tags present in stored metrics
verification: Integration test passes. Pipeline metrics persisted to SQLite correctly.
tests: Mock input → pipeline → local store: metrics in SQLite with correct fields
Stored metrics decode back to original values via MessagePack
Tag index reflects stored metric series
Global tags present in stored metrics

description: Implement store-and-forward buffer — per-output SQLite buffer with transaction model and overflow policies
steps: Create src/buffer/store-forward.ts
Implement StoreForwardBuffer class with SQLite persistence
Implement add(metrics): encode and INSERT into buffer table
Implement beginTransaction(batchSize): SELECT oldest N, return BufferTransaction
Implement BufferTransaction.acceptAll(): DELETE acknowledged metrics
Implement BufferTransaction.keepAll(): no-op (leave for retry)
Implement BufferTransaction.accept(indices) / reject(indices): granular control
Implement overflow policy drop_oldest: DELETE oldest when limit exceeded
Implement overflow policy disk_spill: memory buffer overflows to SQLite
Implement startup recovery: unacknowledged metrics still available after restart
Implement buffer length tracking
Implement close(): flush and close SQLite handle
Implement per-output buffer table naming: buffer_{alias}
Write comprehensive tests
verification: S&F buffer provides at-least-once delivery. Recovery works after simulated crash. All tests pass.
tests: add() persists metrics to SQLite buffer table
beginTransaction() returns oldest N metrics in order
acceptAll() removes all transaction metrics from buffer
keepAll() leaves all metrics for retry (buffer unchanged)
accept(indices) removes selected metrics, keeps others
reject(indices) removes rejected metrics (won't retry)
Buffer limit (drop_oldest): oldest dropped when limit exceeded
disk_spill: memory overflow goes to SQLite without dropping
Recovery: metrics written → close → reopen → metrics still in buffer
Empty buffer: beginTransaction returns empty batch
Buffer length tracks current count accurately
Multiple add() calls → beginTransaction returns all in order
Per-output table naming: different aliases get different tables

description: Integration test: S&F buffer with output — buffer wraps output, handles failures, recovers
steps: Create test/integration/buffer-output.test.ts
Test buffer with mock output that succeeds → metrics delivered and removed from buffer
Test buffer with mock output that fails → metrics kept for retry
Test buffer with mock output that partially fails → granular accept/reject
Test crash recovery: add metrics, simulate crash, reopen, metrics still buffered
verification: Buffer integrates with outputs correctly. Recovery works. All tests pass.
tests: Buffer + successful output: metrics delivered and buffer drained
Buffer + failing output: metrics retained for retry
Buffer + partial failure: accepted removed, rejected removed, rest kept
Crash recovery: buffer survives simulated restart
